name: azure-storage-file-share-ts
description: Azure File Share JavaScript/TypeScript SDK reference for SMB file share operations. Includes examples for creating shares, managing directories, uploading and downloading files, and handling file metadata.
capabilities: Provide TypeScript code examples for Azure File Share SDK operations
Demonstrate authentication methods including connection strings and DefaultAzureCredential
Show file upload and download patterns with streams and buffers
Explain directory and file management operations in Azure Storage
Illustrate SAS token generation for secure access
Document error handling patterns for REST API calls
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Prompt-only documentation skill with no executable code. Contains TypeScript examples for Azure SDK usage. No security risks detected. All code examples are reference documentation demonstrating legitimate Azure Storage File Share operations.
valueStatement: Simplify Azure File Share integration with ready-to-use TypeScript examples. This reference skill provides code patterns for SMB file operations, authentication, and error handling.
userTitle: Manage Azure File Shares with TypeScript
limitations: Some features like SAS generation only work in Node.js not browser
Does not execute code - provides reference documentation only
Requires valid Azure Storage account credentials to use examples

useCases.title: Backend developer integrating Azure File Storage
useCases.description: A developer building a Node.js application needs to store and retrieve files from Azure File Shares using SMB protocol. They need quick reference code for authentication and file operations.

useCases.title: DevOps engineer configuring cloud storage
useCases.description: An engineer setting up automated workflows that interact with Azure File Shares. They need examples for creating shares, setting quotas, and managing directories programmatically.

useCases.title: Full-stack developer migrating file storage
useCases.description: A developer migrating from local file systems to Azure Storage File Shares. They need guidance on upload patterns, stream handling, and error handling for production use.

promptTemplates.title: Basic file upload
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to upload a file to Azure File Share using TypeScript
promptTemplates.scenario: Beginner getting started with Azure File Share operations

promptTemplates.title: List directory contents
promptTemplates.prompt: Provide code to list all files and directories in an Azure File Share directory
promptTemplates.scenario: Exploring file share contents and structure

promptTemplates.title: Stream large file upload
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I upload a large file using streams to Azure File Share in Node.js?

promptTemplates.title: Generate SAS token
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to generate a SAS token for secure file access with specific permissions
promptTemplates.scenario: Implementing secure, time-limited access to files

bestPractices: Use DefaultAzureCredential for production environments instead of hardcoded credentials
Set quotas on shares to prevent unexpected storage costs from unlimited file growth
Use streaming methods for large files to avoid loading entire files into memory
Handle RestError exceptions by checking statusCode for specific error conditions
antiPatterns: Never hardcode storage account keys or connection strings in source code
Avoid using uploadRange for large files when uploadStream is more efficient
Do not skip error handling - Azure operations can fail with network or authentication issues
Never log or expose SAS tokens or storage account keys in error messages

faq.question: What authentication method should I use?
faq.answer: Use connection strings for local development. For production, use DefaultAzureCredential with managed identities for secure, keyless authentication.

faq.question: Can I use this skill in browser applications?
faq.answer: Some features like SAS generation and file operations are Node.js only. Browser applications must use pre-generated SAS tokens for authentication.

faq.question: How do I handle large file uploads?
faq.answer: Use the uploadStream() method with configurable buffer size and concurrency for files larger than 256MB to avoid memory issues.

faq.question: What is the difference between ShareClient and ShareDirectoryClient?
faq.answer: ShareClient operates at the share level for quotas and snapshots. ShareDirectoryClient handles directory and file operations within a share.

faq.question: How do I implement error handling?
faq.answer: Catch RestError exceptions and check the statusCode property. Handle 404 for not found, 409 for conflicts, and 403 for access denied errors.

faq.question: Can I create snapshots of my file shares?
faq.answer: Yes, use shareClient.createSnapshot() to create point-in-time snapshots. Access snapshots with withSnapshot() method and delete with deleteSnapshots option.
